name: UIUC

toefl.note: New scale — exams taken on or after Jan 21, 2026.

toeflLegacy.note: Old scale — exams taken before Jan 21, 2026. All section scores must be 20+.

englishRequirement.note: English proficiency cannot be waived via English Composition courses alone. Accepted paths: TOEFL 5.0+ (new scale, on/after Jan 21 2026) or 100+ (old scale, before Jan 21 2026), IELTS 7.5+, Duolingo 130+, or completing two years of continuous full-time study (12+ credit hours/semester) at a U.S. regionally accredited institution.

requiredCourses.name: Calculus I
requiredCourses.description: Equivalent to MATH 220 or MATH 221 at UIUC.

requiredCourses.name: Calculus III
requiredCourses.description: Equivalent to MATH 241 at UIUC. Required before applying.

requiredCourses.name: General Chemistry I + Lab
requiredCourses.description: Equivalent to CHEM 102 + CHEM 103 at UIUC. AP Chemistry awards lecture credit only — lab must be completed separately.

requiredCourses.name: Computing / Programming Course
requiredCourses.description: One of: CS 101 (Intro Computing), CS 124 (Intro to CS I), SE 101 (Engineering Graphics & Design), or ME 170 (Computer-Aided Design) at UIUC.

notes: In-progress courses are accepted at time of application. A B or better (3.0/4.0) is required in each required/technical course. Average admitted GPA is above 3.5. Source: transferhandbook.illinois.edu/eng/mechanical-engineering/

name: Purdue University

englishRequirement.note: English composition or speech is required as a pre-transfer course. International students must also meet TOEFL 80+, IELTS 6.5+, or Duolingo 105+.

requiredCourses.name: Calculus I
requiredCourses.description: Equivalent to MA 16100 or MA 16500 at Purdue.

requiredCourses.name: Calculus II
requiredCourses.description: Equivalent to MA 16200 or MA 16600 at Purdue.

requiredCourses.name: Advanced Math, Chemistry, or Physics
requiredCourses.description: One advanced course in math, chemistry, or physics.

requiredCourses.name: English Composition or Speech
requiredCourses.description: A college-level English composition or speech course. Must be completed (not in progress) at time of application.

notes: All required courses must be completed and cannot be in progress at time of application. Minimum 3.0 GPA. Source: admissions.purdue.edu
